title: Application CI Business Service Coverage
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" earliest=-1d
| where match(lower(ci_class),"(?i)cmdb_ci_appl|application")
| eval bs=coalesce(business_service, u_business_service)
| eval mapped=if(isnotnull(bs) AND bs!="",1,0)
| stats count as apps, sum(mapped) as mapped_apps by support_group
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*mapped_apps/nullif(apps,0),1)
| sort coverage_pct
description: Applications without a business service mapping weaken BIA-driven prioritization and incident impact statements; coverage metrics align the CMDB with ITIL service asset and configuration practices.
value: Applications without a business service mapping weaken BIA-driven prioritization and incident impact statements; coverage metrics align the CMDB with ITIL service asset and configuration practices.
implementation: (1) Adjust `ci_class` filters to your CMDB application table set; (2) Map whichever field holds the business service reference; (3) Set a minimum `apps` threshold before flagging low `coverage_pct` groups to avoid noise from tiny teams.
visualization: Bar chart (coverage % by support group), Table (unmapped application CIs), Gauge (overall coverage %).
knownFalsePositives: Shifts in ticket and workflow data during large incidents, process or field changes, holiday coverage, and after big rollouts; spot-check a sample in the source system before you treat the trend as a people problem.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci"` (`ci_class`, `business_service`, `u_business_service`, `name`, `support_group`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Adjust `ci_class` filters to your CMDB application table set; (2) Map whichever field holds the business service reference; (3) Set a minimum `apps` threshold before flagging low `coverage_pct` groups to avoid noise from tiny teams.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" earliest=-1d
| where match(lower(ci_class),"(?i)cmdb_ci_appl|application")
| eval bs=coalesce(business_service, u_business_service)
| eval mapped=if(isnotnull(bs) AND bs!="",1,0)
| stats count as apps, sum(mapped) as mapped_apps by support_group
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*mapped_apps/nullif(apps,0),1)
| sort coverage_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Application CI Business Service Coverage** ‚Äî Applications without a business service mapping weaken BIA-driven prioritization and incident impact statements; coverage metrics align the CMDB with ITIL service asset and configuration practices.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci"` (`ci_class`, `business_service`, `u_business_service`, `name`, `support_group`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:cmdb_ci. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(ci_class),"(?i)cmdb_ci_appl|application")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **bs** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **mapped** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by support_group** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **coverage_pct** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow configuration item or related CMDB view. Spot-check a few CIs, owners, and relationship fields, then confirm discovery dates if we measure freshness.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (coverage % by support group), Table (unmapped application CIs), Gauge (overall coverage %).
grandmaExplanation: We see which applications tie to which business service, so impact maps and priority lines stay true when we break or fix things.
